question: A Camunda 8.7 application uses the Spring Zeebe SDK. The team upgrades the cluster to Camunda 8.8 with no code changes and plans to upgrade to 8.10 later. What does the docs recommend they do before that 8.10 upgrade?

options.text: Migrate to the Camunda Spring Boot Starter — the Spring Zeebe SDK is removed in version 8.10.
Pin the Spring Zeebe SDK to its 8.8 release; it is officially supported through 8.10.
Drop the starter entirely and call the gRPC API directly — only the raw gRPC client is part of the public API.
Stay on gRPC explicitly — the new Camunda Spring Boot Starter does not support gRPC at all, only REST.

a.text: The migration note on the docs is explicit: "Spring Zeebe SDK will be removed in version 8.10. Migrate before upgrading to 8.10 to avoid breaking changes." The replacement is the Camunda Spring Boot Starter.

b.text: The SDK is being removed, not extended; pinning a version cannot keep it usable against 8.10.

c.text: The Camunda Spring Boot Starter is itself part of the Camunda 8 public API; there is no need to drop it.

d.text: REST is the _default_ protocol of the new starter, but gRPC is configurable — both are supported.

explanation: The Camunda Spring Boot Starter replaces the Spring Zeebe SDK as of 8.8 and is the official path forward. The SDK is removed in 8.10, so applications on the SDK must migrate before that upgrade. The new starter uses the Camunda Java Client under the hood and supports REST as the default protocol with gRPC configurable.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - Getting started
